Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Turing Award Honors Bennett and Brassard for Quantum Information Science 58 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. FFmpeg 101 (2024) 167 upvotes, 5 comments
  3. Attention Residuals 213 upvotes, 29 comments
  4. Linux Applications Programming by Example: The Fundamental APIs (2nd Edition) 138 upvotes, 17 comments
  5. Ghostling 270 upvotes, 52 comments
  6. Mamba-3 220 upvotes, 43 comments
  7. Senior European journalist suspended over AI-generated quotes 11 upvotes, 0 comments
  8. Grafeo – A fast, lean, embeddable graph database built in Rust 30 upvotes, 4 comments
  9. Blocking Internet Archive Won't Stop AI, but Will Erase Web's Historical Record 300 upvotes, 86 comments
  10. Meta's Omnilingual MT for 1,600 Languages 68 upvotes, 16 comments
  11. Fujifilm X RAW STUDIO webapp clone 117 upvotes, 42 comments
  12. OpenCode – Open source AI coding agent 1066 upvotes, 524 comments
  13. Molly Guard 169 upvotes, 74 comments
  14. The Los Angeles Aqueduct Is Wild 396 upvotes, 197 comments
  15. The worst volume control UI in the world (2017) 186 upvotes, 89 comments
  16. AI Team OS – Turn Claude Code into a Self-Managing AI Team 21 upvotes, 6 comments
  17. How HN: Ironkernel – Python expressions, Rust parallel 23 upvotes, 7 comments
  18. Apple Announces New Mac Sales Record Following MacBook Neo Launch 22 upvotes, 7 comments
  19. 404 Deno CEO not found 75 upvotes, 37 comments
  20. First science from private Moon lander challenges lunar divide 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  21. An industrial piping contractor on Claude Code [video] 91 upvotes, 50 comments
  22. Some Things Just Take Time 31 upvotes, 13 comments
  23. We rewrote our Rust WASM parser in TypeScript and it got faster 260 upvotes, 170 comments
  24. Cryptography in Home Entertainment (2004) 69 upvotes, 38 comments
  25. Padel Chess – tactical simulator for padel 63 upvotes, 35 comments
  26. A Japanese glossary of chopsticks faux pas (2022) 376 upvotes, 293 comments
  27. ZJIT removes redundant object loads and stores 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  28. The Story of Marina Abramovic and Ulay (2020) 31 upvotes, 18 comments
  29. Books of the Century by Le Monde 17 upvotes, 9 comments
  30. Show HN: A deterministic middleware to compress LLM prompts by 50-80% 3 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 12:30am, 22 March 2026. Source here.
